data.title: Alibaba Profit Crashes 67% — But Analysts Are Doubling Down. Here's Why.
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) stock slipped 7% on Thursday after reporting its Fiscal Q3 results, with adjusted net income falling 67% year-over-year to RMB16.7 billion, well below expectations. The sharp profit drop was driven by higher spending on e-commerce and AI.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, analysts are looking past the miss, pointing to a strong 36% jump in cloud revenue and early signs that pricing pressure in China's cloud market may be easing, which could support a recovery in margins later this year.

BofA: Q3 Profit Miss, but Growth Remains Strong

Following the Q3 print, BofA Securities analyst Joyce Ju maintained a Buy rating and a $180 price target on Alibaba. The analyst noted that some weakness in the core e-commerce business was already expected due to a softer macro environment. However, the profit miss was larger than expected, mainly due to higher spending.

Alibaba is investing more in improving its e-commerce platform and expanding its AI capabilities. While this weighed on near-term margins, BofA sees it as a strategic move to support long-term growth.

Importantly, Ju highlighted that the 36% growth in cloud revenue was the "cleanest" part of the report. While e-commerce remains in a transition phase, she noted that the cloud business is now showing improving pricing power — something investors have been waiting to see.

The firm believes this strength, along with ongoing investments, positions Alibaba for better growth and margin recovery ahead.

Goldman and Morgan Stanley Focus on Long-Term Growth

Analysts at Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley also remained constructive on the stock. They noted that the profit decline was mainly due to higher reinvestment, not weaker demand.

Both firms view Alibaba's increased spending as a strategic move to strengthen its position in e-commerce and AI. While this may weigh on earnings in the near term, it could support stronger growth over time.

Is Alibaba Stock a Good Buy Right Now?   

Analysts remain bullish about Alibaba's stock trajectory. With eight Buy ratings and one Hold rating, BABA stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. Also, the average Alibaba price target of $197.86 implies about 47.18% upside potential from current levels. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: SanDisk Stock Jumps as Citi Analyst Hikes Forecast, Says 'Data Center NAND Bit Demand' Is Through the Roof
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock jumped 2.5% higher by market close on March 19, 2026. This happened after Citi (C) significantly raised its price target for the company yesterday. Analyst Asiya Merchant updated her forecast to $875, up from her previous target of $750, while keeping a Buy rating. The move follows strong results from other memory chip companies, which suggest the storage market is entering a period of extreme growth driven by artificial intelligence.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AI Demand Drives Need for Data Center Storage

Asiya Merchant highlighted that the demand for the memory used in high-speed storage is growing faster than expected. In her report from yesterday, she noted that "data center NAND bit demand is accelerating on AI use cases." This shift is very good for SanDisk because it makes the high-performance drives that these data centers need to run AI models. Merchant expects the industry's total supply to grow by about 20% in 2026, but she believes that demand will stay much higher than what factories can actually produce.

Severe Supply Shortage Supports Favorable Pricing Environment

One of the main reasons for the higher price target is the expectation that memory chips will stay in short supply. Merchant wrote in her latest report that "demand significantly exceeds available supply." Because there are not enough chips to go around, SanDisk has the power to keep its prices high. This helps the company's profit margins grow. She also pointed out that while competitors are starting to build new factories, those won't be ready until late 2028, which keeps the market tight for the next few years.

Trade SNDK with leverage

SanDisk Successfully Manages a 'Dynamic Environment'

Even with high demand, SanDisk is being careful about its growth. In her March 19 update, Merchant explained that the company is successfully managing a "dynamic environment" as it moves toward its long-term goals. Instead of just trying to sell as many chips as possible right now, SanDisk is working to secure long-term contracts. This focus on the future is a major reason why Citi believes the stock is a strong buy as the current "memory supercycle" continues.

Is SanDisk a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street currently maintains a Strong Buy consensus for SanDisk, with 12 analysts calling it a Buy and three rating it a Hold. Even so, the average 12-month SNDK price target sits at $696.67, which is about 10% lower than the current price. This gap suggests that many experts believe the stock is priced too high right now. This cautious view remains in place even though SanDisk is expected to see a massive jump in its profits very soon.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market Review Today: SPY, QQQ Stumble as Iran Escalates War; U.S. Weighs Options to Cool Oil Prices
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Thursday as heightened tensions across the Middle East threatened to further disrupt the oil supply chain and fuel higher inflation.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Iran launched attacks on Saudi Aramco's Samref refinery, Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City, and Kuwait Petroleum Corporation's Mina al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed.
  • Brent crude futures (BZ), the international benchmark for oil, reversed gains after the U.S. green-lighted the sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum products loaded on vessels as of March 12.
  • The U.S. is considering lifting sanctions on Iranian oil and initiating a second Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) release. Removing the sanctions would allow oil to "flow into Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia, Japan, India — who have been good actors in this," said Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
  • JPMorgan lowered its year-end S&P 500 (SPX) price target to 7,200 from 7,500, citing recession risks from rising oil prices. The firm's target is well below the average Wall Street estimate of 7,600.
  • Micron (MU) closed lower, despite reporting a double beat on its fiscal second-quarter revenue and adjusted EPS and raising guidance.
  • Tesla (TSLA) faced pressure after the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) upgraded its investigation into the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) service.
  • Goldman Sachs (GS) and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) launched instruments enabling hedge funds to bet against the private credit market.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Energy was the best-performing sector for a third consecutive day as the oil supply chain in the Middle East faces new threats from Iran. With higher oil prices, producers and refiners stand to benefit from higher revenues and stronger profit margins.

Trade QQQ with leverage

Several stocks led the energy sector's gains, including:

  • Baker Hughes (BKR)
  • SLB (SLB)
  • APA (APA)
  • Phillips 66 (PSX)
  • Targa Resources (TRGP)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, materials was the worst-performing sector due to inflation risks from higher energy prices, as rising input and transportation costs could pressure margins for companies across the sector. In addition, inflation fears have lowered rate cut odds, weighing on commodity producers and mining companies.

Notable materials stocks trading lower include:

  • Newmont Mining (NEM)
  • Packaging Corp of America (PKG)
  • Ecolab (ECL)
  • PPG Industries (PPG)
  • Sherwin-Williams (SHW)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Jeff Bezos Looks to Raise $100B for a New AI Fund
data.description:
Jeff Bezos, the founder of tech giant Amazon (AMZN), is in early talks to raise a $100 billion fund that will focus on buying manufacturing companies and using AI to speed up automation, according to The Wall Street Journal. In fact, Bezos has been meeting with major asset managers and traveling to regions such as the Middle East and Singapore to speak with sovereign wealth funds. Notably, the fund would target industries such as chipmaking, defense, and aerospace, and could make it one of the largest investment vehicles of its kind.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, this plan is closely connected to Project Prometheus, a startup for which Bezos now serves as co-CEO. Interestingly, the company is building AI systems that can simulate how the physical world works. This includes predicting how materials react under pressure or how airflow moves around objects. Bezos intends to use this technology to improve the efficiency of companies owned by the fund.

Trade TSLA with leverage

Importantly, this move shows that AI is moving toward real-world industrial use rather than just software or chatbots. Indeed, companies are now investing heavily in AI for robotics, manufacturing, and physical systems. For example, Uber (UBER) founder Travis Kalanick is launching a manufacturing-focused AI venture, while Elon Musk is pushing robotics at Tesla (TSLA) with self-driving and humanoid projects.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 40 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $280 per share implies 34.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Micron Stock Forecast Soars as Analysts See 'No Indication of Slowing Momentum' thanks to 'Tight Supply'
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) is seeing an influx of positive news after several top-rated analysts significantly raised their price targets on March 19, 2026. This surge in confidence comes as the company continues to beat earnings expectations and reports massive demand for its memory chips. Experts from Barclays (BCS), KeyBanc ($, and Stifel (SF) are all pointing toward a strong future, with some predicting the stock could rise by over 50% from current levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Barclays Sees Strong Growth Driven by Tight Supply

Analyst Thomas O'Malley from Barclays raised his price target for Micron to $675 on March 19, 2026. This is a huge jump from his previous target of $450 and implies an upside of nearly 52%. O'Malley highlighted that the company is benefiting from a "strong quarter and guidance supported by tight supply and demand conditions" for both DRAM and NAND memory through 2026. He also noted that developments in AI remain a major boost for the memory market and that he sees "no indication of slowing momentum in the near term."

KeyBanc Highlights Tight Supply and High-Bandwidth Memory

John Vinh of KeyBanc also raised his target price on March 19, 2026, moving it from $450 to $600. This suggests a potential gain of 35%. Vinh pointed out that memory pricing is rising due to "tight industry supply and a favorable mix of high-bandwidth memory (HBM)." He noted that revenues from HBM are growing quickly and are now approaching an annual run rate of $8 billion. This specialized memory is essential for AI applications, which is a key reason for his bullish outlook.

Stifel Reiterates Buy Rating with $550 Target

Analyst Brian Chin from Stifel Nicolaus maintained his Buy rating and raised his price target to $550 on March 19, 2026. This target implies a potential increase of about 24%. Chin's updated outlook reflects the broader optimism across Wall Street, where the consensus remains a strong "Buy." He believes that Micron is well-positioned to capitalize on the current memory cycle, especially as the company's financial results continue to exceed market expectations.

Why the Memory Market is Entering a "Stratospheric" Phase

The latest earnings report from Micron has drawn a lot of attention because the results significantly outperformed what the market was expecting. Revenue reached $23.86 billion, which was 24% higher than predicted. Analysts are now saying that investors may have been reading the memory market wrong and that the company is seeing a "stratospheric increase in earnings expectations." Since Micron has already sold all of its high-speed memory chips for the next two years, the company is now in a position to grow faster than ever before.

Is Micron a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Micron stock based on 25 Buy and two Hold recommendations. The average 12-month MU stock price target of $536.41 indicates 20.74% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Bracing for Stagflation? These 2 Stocks Offer Mouth-Watering Upside
data.description:
Escalating tensions in the Middle East, a spike in crude prices, and signs of fatigue in the U.S. labor market are fueling fresh analyst chatter about the return of 1970s-style stagflation — a backdrop of surging prices and stalling economic activity.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This article flags two stocks that offer massive upside: Gossamer Bio (GOSS) and Insulet (PODD). Both are healthcare companies, which are considered among the strongest portfolio defenses in periods of economic slowdown or recession. This is because health services are critical to everyday survival and have inelastic demand.

These stocks were selected for their impressive upside potential and Buy ratings from analysts. They were also picked because they are some of the top holdings of ETFs highlighted here and here as providing shelter to investors in a stagflationary environment.

Gossamer Bio (GOSS)

Gossamer is an American biotech firm that specializes in the development and commercialization of treatments for diseases that affect the immune system, cause inflammation, and other health problems such as cancer. The California-based company's current lead drug candidate is seralutinib, an inhaled treatment for pulmonary arterial hypertension.

While Gossamer's shares have fallen more than 85% since the start of the year, the top six Wall Street analysts covering the stock consider it a Moderate Buy. This comes with an average price target of $4.25, implying an explosive 840% upside from current trading levels.

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Tick Up after Back-to-Back Market Losses
data.description:
U.S. stock futures inched higher on Thursday evening after the market logged its second consecutive losing session. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.13%, 0.17%, and 0.16%, respectively, at 6:27 p.m. EDT on March 19.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Have high conviction in QQQ? Leverage your view

Major stock indices ended lower on Thursday, trimming steep losses late in the session as oil prices pulled back from their intraday highs. The Dow slipped 0.4%, while the Nasdaq and S&P 500 each fell 0.3%.

Further, inflation concerns weighed on market sentiment. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell warned that surging energy costs could pressure inflation expectations, leading traders to price in higher odds of zero rate cuts for the remainder of 2026.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: GE Aerospace to Invest €110M in Europe to Boost Manufacturing Capacity; Stock Drops 3%
data.description:
GE Aerospace (GE) is expanding its global production footprint with plans to invest more than €110 million across its European manufacturing sites this year. The goal is to boost capacity, advance manufacturing capabilities, and improve delivery performance for both commercial and defense customers. GE stock declined over 3% on Thursday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As part of the expansion, GE Aerospace plans to hire more than 1,000 new workers across Europe in 2026 to strengthen its manufacturing base and support local workforce growth.

Where the Money Is Going

A large part of the investment will go toward new engine test cells, advanced machining systems, expanded additive manufacturing, and building and infrastructure upgrades. These improvements will support a wide range of programs, including commercial narrowbody and widebody engines, as well as military fighter jet and helicopter engines.

Beyond manufacturing, GE Aerospace will also invest about €40 million in its European maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) and component repair facilities this year. This is part of a broader $1 billion global MRO investment program first announced in 2024.

GE Aerospace's Investment Drive

The latest spending news comes just days after GE Aerospace announced plans to invest $1 billion in its U.S. manufacturing sites to boost engine deliveries and increase parts production.

The move marked another major commitment from the company, following a similar $1 billion investment announced in 2025, as it works to meet rising global demand for aircraft engines and components.

GE Aerospace's large spending plans in its U.S. and European facilities show confidence in long‑term demand. Also, they are expected to help the company deliver better performance, faster turnaround times, and improved reliability for airlines and defense customers.

Is GE Stock a Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on GE stock based on 13 Buy recommendations assigned in the past three months. The average GE Aerospace stock price target of $373.42 indicates 28.05% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Google (GOOGL) Strikes Power Deals to Cut Data Center Usage During Peak Demand
data.description:
Google (GOOGL) said it has signed agreements with five U.S. electric utilities to reduce its electricity usage during periods of peak demand. This move comes as the tech company looks for ways to secure enough power for its fast-growing data centers, which are critical for AI development. In fact, limited power supply and slow infrastructure buildouts have made it increasingly difficult for large tech companies to access the energy they need.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As a result, companies are starting to take more creative approaches in order to manage power constraints. In some cases, tech firms have even considered building their own power plants or restarting old nuclear facilities to meet demand. This is especially important for Google because its agreements use a "demand response" model, in which the company agrees to temporarily reduce electricity usage at certain data centers when the grid is under heavy stress.

Under these contracts, Google may reduce up to 1 gigawatt of electricity usage during peak periods, which is roughly enough to power 750,000 homes. This approach helps utilities manage spikes in demand, which typically occur during extreme weather, while also lowering the risk of blackouts and making the grid more stable overall. However, it makes Google's need to build its own power sources even more urgent.

Are GOOGL Shares a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 26 Buys and six Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average GOOGL price target of $376.57 per share implies 23% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
